# Lawly — AI-Powered Legal Notice Generator for Indian Consumers

> Lawly helps Indian consumers generate professional, legally-sound consumer protection notices in minutes using AI. Backed by 25+ Indian statutes including the Consumer Protection Act 2019.

## What Lawly Does
Lawly is India's first AI-powered legal notice generator purpose-built for consumer complaints. Users describe their grievance in plain language, and Lawly's multi-agent AI pipeline produces a fully-formatted legal notice citing the exact statutes, precedents, and remedies applicable to their case.

## Core Capabilities
- **AI Legal Notice Generation**: Multi-agent pipeline analyzes complaints, identifies applicable laws, and generates professional legal notices
- **Indian Consumer Law Expertise**: Deep coverage of CPA 2019, Sale of Goods Act 1930, E-Commerce Rules 2020, RERA 2016, IT Act 2000, DGCA CAR, RBI circulars, IRDAI regulations, FSSA 2006, and 15+ more statutes
- **Three Service Tiers**: Self-Send (₹199), Professional Review, and Lawyer-Drafted (₹599) options
- **Instant PDF Generation**: Download properly formatted legal notices as PDFs ready to send via registered post
- **Evidence Analysis**: AI scores and organizes supporting evidence for stronger notices
- **Jurisdiction Detection**: Automatically identifies the correct consumer forum based on claim value and location

## Use Cases Covered
- UPI & Payment Failures (RBI TAT circular, Payment & Settlement Systems Act)
- Dark Patterns on Apps (CCPA Guidelines 2023, CPA 2019 §2(47))
- Ed-Tech Refunds (Byju's, Unacademy — E-Commerce Rules 2020, UGC Guidelines)
- Flight Cancellation & Delay (DGCA CAR Section 3, Series M, Part IV)
- RERA Builder Delays (RERA 2016 §18 — SBI MCLR + 2% interest)
- Insurance Claim Rejections (IRDAI Regulation 17(7), Insurance Act §45)
- Wrong CIBIL / Credit Score (CICRA 2005 §15)
- Expired Food — Quick Commerce (FSSA 2006 §59, FSSAI)
- Cheque Bounce (Negotiable Instruments Act §138)
- Recovery of Money/Dues
- Defamation Notices
- Defective Products (Chapter VI Product Liability, CPA 2019)

## Who It's For
- Indian consumers with unresolved complaints against companies
- People facing issues with e-commerce refunds, defective products, deficient services, insurance denials, real estate delays, or banking disputes
- Anyone who needs a legal notice but cannot afford traditional lawyer fees (₹5,000–₹25,000)

## Pricing
- Self-Send Tier: ₹199 — AI-generated notice, user sends it themselves
- Lawyer-Drafted: ₹599 — Full attorney involvement with Lawly AI as a drafting assistant

- What is a Legal Notice in India (explainer): https://lawly.store/what-is-a-legal-notice
- How to Reply to a Legal Notice in India: https://lawly.store/reply-to-legal-notice

- Legal Notice for Insurance Claim Rejected: https://lawly.store/legal-notice-insurance-claim-rejected

- Legal Notice for UPI Payment Failure: https://lawly.store/legal-notice-upi-payment-failure
- Legal Notice for Flight Cancellation (DGCA): https://lawly.store/legal-notice-flight-cancellation
